txt: What is the chemical symbol for gold?
why: Gold's symbol Au comes from the Latin word 'aurum', meaning gold.

txt: How many bones are in the adult human body?
why: An adult human body has exactly 206 bones; babies are born with around 270 that fuse over time.

txt: Which planet has the most moons in our solar system?
why: Saturn leads with 146 confirmed moons as of 2024, surpassing Jupiter's 95.

txt: What is the approximate speed of light in a vacuum?
why: Light travels at approximately 299,792 km/s in a vacuum, commonly rounded to 300,000 km/s.

txt: Which element has the highest melting point of all known elements?
why: Tungsten (W) has the highest melting point of any pure element at 3,422 degrees Celsius.

txt: In which year did World War II end?
why: World War II ended in 1945: Germany surrendered in May and Japan in September.

txt: Who was the first President of the United States?
why: George Washington was inaugurated as the first U.S. President on April 30, 1789.

txt: The Berlin Wall fell in which year?
why: The Berlin Wall fell on November 9, 1989, after the GDR announced open borders.

txt: Which ancient wonder was located in Alexandria, Egypt?
why: The Lighthouse of Alexandria (Pharos) stood at the entrance to Alexandria's harbour.

txt: The Peace of Westphalia in 1648 ended which major European conflict?
why: The Peace of Westphalia (1648) ended the Thirty Years War and reshaped European sovereignty.

txt: What is the longest river in the world?
why: The Nile River at approximately 6,650 km is the longest river in the world.

txt: Which country has the largest land area in the world?
why: Russia covers approximately 17.1 million square kilometres, making it the largest country by area.

txt: Which ocean is the deepest in the world?
why: The Pacific Ocean contains the Mariana Trench, the deepest point on Earth at approximately 11,034 m.

txt: The Strait of Malacca connects which two bodies of water?
opt: Andaman Sea and South China Sea
Bay of Bengal and Java Sea
why: The Strait of Malacca connects the Andaman Sea (Indian Ocean) to the South China Sea (Pacific Ocean).

txt: Which African country has the most UNESCO World Heritage Sites?
why: Egypt leads African nations with 7 UNESCO World Heritage Sites, including Giza and Abu Simbel.
